summary: Removed the `--wip` configure flag, made `-DWIP` part of the default compiler flags, and explicitly unlinked many work-in-progress fighter objects from the matching build by changing their `Object(Matching, ...)` entries to `Object(NonMatching, ...)`. The PR also corrected a small GALE01 split boundary in `.sdata2` between `melee/gm/gm_1A36.c` and `melee/gr/ground.c`.
what_changed: Deleted the `--wip` argparse option from `configure.py`; WIP is no longer an optional command-line build mode.
Added `-DWIP` unconditionally to `cflags_base`, while leaving the existing `-DMUST_MATCH` behavior controlled by `--non-matching`.
Removed the conditional `if args.wip: cflags_base.append("-DWIP")` block.

Changed many fighter-related object declarations from `Object(Matching, ...)` to `Object(NonMatching, ...)`, including `melee/ft/ftaction.c`, `melee/ft/ft_0881.c`, several `ftCommon` files, Captain Falcon special files, Link special N, Peach float/special low, and other fighter main/common files.
Adjusted `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `melee/gm/gm_1A36.c` `.sdata2` ends at `0x804DAC80` instead of `0x804DAC90`, and `melee/gr/ground.c` `.sdata2` starts at `0x804DAC80` instead of `0x804DAC90`.
smart_moves: Moved WIP handling from a global opt-in macro flag to explicit object linkage classification: WIP code can be compiled under `-DWIP`, while unfinished files are kept out of the matching link with `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Preserved the existing matching/nonmatching build split by using the established `Matching` versus `NonMatching` object mechanism rather than adding another build-mode axis.
Kept the split file consistent with the changed object layout by moving the `.sdata2` boundary by 0x10 bytes between adjacent files instead of leaving stale section ownership.
decomp_lessons: For decomp build hygiene, prefer marking unfinished translation units as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` over maintaining a separate `--wip` build flag that can hide object-linkage state.
If WIP source guards are broadly needed for compilation, defining `-DWIP` globally is less ambiguous when matching participation is controlled separately by object status.
When changing which objects are treated as matching/nonmatching, check adjacent split boundaries, especially small data sections such as `.sdata2`, because ownership can shift by small amounts.
A file being compilable is not the same as being safe to link into the matching build; configure object classification should encode that distinction.
naming_conventions: `Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c")` denotes objects intended for the matching build.
`Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c")` denotes objects excluded from the matching build and associated with non-matching/WIP code paths.
Fighter common files use the `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_*.c` naming pattern.
Character-specific fighter files use short character prefixes such as `ftCa_` for Captain Falcon, `ftLk_` for Link, and `ftPe_` for Peach.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Demote nonmatching or WIP translation units in `configure.py` rather than relying on conditional compilation to make them disappear.
Keep `-DMUST_MATCH` tied to the absence of `--non-matching`; this PR did not alter that core matching guard.
When adjusting split ownership, preserve contiguous section ranges: `gm_1A36.c` `.sdata2` now ends exactly where `ground.c` `.sdata2` begins, at `0x804DAC80`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that re-promoted these `Object(NonMatching, ...)` fighter files back to `Object(Matching, ...)`.
Search for remaining uses of `#ifdef WIP` or `-DWIP` after this PR to understand how WIP guards evolved.
Check subsequent split changes around `gm_1A36.c` and `ground.c` `.sdata2` to confirm the 0x10-byte boundary adjustment remained stable.
